site.title: NRL TipBot Briefing 2026
site.subhead: Round 19 live tracker and Prince_of_Penrith top-two chase
site.intro: A weekly NRL command board for sharper tipping, cleaner ladder strategy, and banking any wins for charity.

meta.description: Codex-managed NRL TipBot dashboard for Round 19 live tracking, Prince_of_Penrith ladder strategy, and Mark Hughes Foundation ledger discipline.

ticker: prince_of_penrith is 3rd on 194 points and 5 points behind 2nd on the supplied round 19 ladder screenshot
round 19 card is 3 correct 4 missed after a user reported storm win over the titans
warriors sharks and storm landed but raiders roosters rabbitohs and cowboys beat our leans
goal is top 2 before finals not wild chasing

featurePanels.title: Ladder Target
items: User-supplied Round 19 ladder screenshot shows Prince_of_Penrith 3rd on 194 points.
Second place is Kristy W13 on 199, so the actionable target is a 5-point swing before finals.
First place NourilM is on 211, a 17-point gap; that is a longer chase and should not drive reckless weekly tactics.
The board now prioritises controlled differentials that can catch 2nd without blowing up the floor.

featurePanels.title: Round 19 Read
items: Round 19 has moved from preview to damage-control tracking.
Correct: Warriors over Wests Tigers, Sharks over Dolphins and Storm over Titans.
Misses so far: Bulldogs over Raiders, Eels over Roosters, Knights over Rabbitohs and Sea Eagles over Cowboys.
Storm over Titans was marked correct from the user-reported final result; the exact score still needs official cross-checking.
The human note is fair: backing Storm was the rational tip, but the Titans underdog push was still easy to cheer.

featurePanels.title: Top-Two Strategy
items: Do not chase the 5-point gap by tipping every underdog; that is how a top-three position becomes 7th.
Use one or two controlled differentials per full round, ideally in games where team-list news gives us a real edge.
Protect obvious heavy favourites when the top two are likely to take them too.
Attack games where the public follows reputation but late mail points the other way.

featurePanels.title: Watchlist
items: Round 20 stays watchlist-only until Team List Tuesday; no early locks.
Start each week by estimating what NourilM and Kristy W13 are likely to tip, then choose where to match and where to split.
Track joker users around positions 6-14 because they can jump fast and change the risk profile.
Keep the charity ledger separate from tipping-comp aggression.

betCard.title: Round 19 Charity Bet
betCard.kickoff: Round runs Friday 10 July to Sunday 12 July 2026
why: The Round 19 tipping card has taken damage and should not be converted into a chase bet after the fact.
Storm was the best raw candidate, but no TAB price and stake were recorded before kickoff.
A charity bet only becomes official after explicit user confirmation before kickoff.
risks: Using a bad tipping round as betting motivation would mix two ledgers that must stay separate.
Round 19 post-Origin volatility has already punished several leans.
Any future charity play still needs recorded odds, stake, match, and explicit confirmation before kickoff.

charity.title: Mark Hughes Foundation Ledger
charity.summary: All profits are earmarked for the Mark Hughes Foundation.
charity.nextDonation: After a meaningful positive bank is built

watchlist: Prince_of_Penrith needs a 5-point swing to catch 2nd, not a miracle card to catch 1st immediately.
Round 20 must start with team-list verification and likely-rival-tip modelling.
Use controlled differentials only where late mail or matchup context gives real edge.
Do not let the Round 19 miss cluster force an aggressive charity-bet decision.
Storm v Titans exact score still needs official confirmation before publishing the final score line.

sections.title: Situation Report
sections.heading: The ladder chase is now explicit
lines: As of Sunday, 12 July 2026, the user-supplied Round 19 comp ladder shows Prince_of_Penrith in 3rd on 194 points.
Kristy W13 is 2nd on 199, so the near-term goal is a 5-point swing before finals.
NourilM is 1st on 211; catching 1st is possible only through sustained weekly edge, not panic tipping.
Round 19 has been updated into live tracking mode rather than preview mode.

sections.title: Bet Analysis
sections.heading: No charity chase after a bad tipping card
lines: The Mark Hughes Foundation ledger remains 3-2 with $31.50 invested, $32.03 returned and +$0.53 settled profit.
No Round 19 charity result should be added without a pre-recorded stake, price and explicit charity-bet confirmation.
Round 19 tipping-comp losses are useful data, not a reason to risk the charity ledger.
If Storm lands, record it as a tipping-card result only unless a confirmed charity bet slip exists.

sections.title: Top-Two Plan
sections.heading: How Prince_of_Penrith gets to 2nd before finals
lines: Protect the base: match obvious high-confidence favourites where leaders are also likely to tip them.
Find one or two edge-based splits per round, preferably late-mail-driven and not just emotional underdogs.
Before lockout, estimate likely tips for NourilM and Kristy W13 and mark each game as match, split, or avoid.
The 5-point gap to 2nd can be closed with two good differential weeks; it does not require a reckless full-card gamble.
Joker status below the top five matters because chasing users can create sudden pressure from behind.

sections.title: Round 20 Prep
sections.heading: No early locks until Team List Tuesday
lines: Round 20 should not be locked from fixture names alone.
Use NRL team lists and late mail first, then build a top-two chase card.
Prioritise games where public confidence is likely to be stale after Round 19 upsets.
Add a leader-differential column to the next TipBot update so we can decide where Prince_of_Penrith should match or split.
